title: NurbsCurve3D Object
description: Transient 3D NURBS curve. A transient NURBS curve is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient 3D NURBS curves are used as a wrapper to work with raw 3D NURBS curve information. They are created statically using one of the create methods of the NurbsCurve3D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this NurbsCurve3D object.

methods.name: createNonRational
methods.description: Creates a transient 3D NURBS non-rational b-spline object.

methods.name: createRational
methods.description: Creates a transient 3D NURBS rational b-spline object.

methods.name: extract
methods.description: Defines a new NURBS curve that is the subset of this NURBS curve in the parameter range of [startParam, endParam]

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets the data that defines a transient 3D NURBS rational b-spline object.

methods.name: merge
methods.description: Define a new NURBS curve that is the result of combining this NURBS curve with another NURBS curve.

methods.name: reverse
methods.description: Reverses the orientation of the curve so the start and end points are swapped. The shape of the curve remains unchanged. This is especially useful to prepare the curves to use with the merge method.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets the data that defines a transient 3D NURBS rational b-spline object.

properties.name: controlPointCount
properties.description: Gets the number of control points that define the curve.

properties.name: controlPoints
properties.description: Returns an array of Point3D objects that define the control points of the curve.

properties.name: curveType
properties.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

properties.name: degree
properties.description: Returns the degree of the curve.

properties.name: evaluator
properties.description: Returns an evaluator object that lets you perform additional evaluations on the curve.

properties.name: isClosed
properties.description: Gets if the curve is closed.

properties.name: isPeriodic
properties.description: Gets if the curve is periodic.

properties.name: isRational
properties.description: Gets if the curve is rational or non-rational type.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: knotCount
properties.description: Returns the knot count of the curve.

properties.name: knots
properties.description: Returns an array of numbers that define the knot vector of the curve.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: NurbsCurve3D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.NurbsCurve3D.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::NurbsCurve3D::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: NurbsCurve3D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this NurbsCurve3D object.

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var.copy()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var->copy();

copy.return_description: Returns an independent copy of this NurbsCurve3D.

createNonRational.title: NurbsCurve3D.createNonRational Method
createNonRational.description: Creates a transient 3D NURBS non-rational b-spline object.

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.NurbsCurve3D.createNonRational(controlPoints, degree, knots, isPeriodic)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::NurbsCurve3D::createNonRational(controlPoints, degree, knots, isPeriodic);

createNonRational.return_description: Returns the new NurbsCurve3D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: controlPoints
parameters.description: An array of control point that define the path of the spline.

parameters.name: degree
parameters.description: The degree of curvature of the spline.

parameters.name: knots
parameters.description: An array of numbers that define the knot vector of the spline. The knots is an array of (>=degree + N + 1) numbers, where N is the number of control points.

parameters.name: isPeriodic
parameters.description: A bool specifying if the spline is to be Periodic. A periodic spline has a start point and end point that meet forming a closed loop.

createRational.title: NurbsCurve3D.createRational Method
createRational.description: Creates a transient 3D NURBS rational b-spline object.

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.NurbsCurve3D.createRational(controlPoints, degree, knots, weights, isPeriodic)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::NurbsCurve3D::createRational(controlPoints, degree, knots, weights, isPeriodic);

createRational.return_description: Returns the new NurbsCurve3D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: controlPoints
parameters.description: An array of control point that define the path of the spline.

parameters.name: degree
parameters.description: The degree of curvature of the spline.

parameters.name: knots
parameters.description: An array of numbers that define the knot vector of the spline. The knots is an array of (>=degree + N + 1) numbers, where N is the number of control points.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: An array of numbers that define the weight at each control point.

parameters.name: isPeriodic
parameters.description: A bool specifying if the spline is to be Periodic. A periodic curve has a start point and end point that meet (with curvature continuity) forming a closed loop.

extract.title: NurbsCurve3D.extract Method
extract.description: Defines a new NURBS curve that is the subset of this NURBS curve in the parameter range of [startParam, endParam]

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var.extract(startParam, endParam)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var->extract(startParam, endParam);

parameters.name: startParam
parameters.description: The parameter position that defines the start of the subset.

parameters.name: endParam
parameters.description: The parameter position that defines the end of the subset.

getData.title: NurbsCurve3D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets the data that defines a transient 3D NURBS rational b-spline object.

syntax.python: (returnValue, controlPoints, degree, knots, isRational, weights, isPeriodic) = nurbsCurve3D_var.getData()
syntax.cpp: #include getData(controlPoints, degree, knots, isRational, weights, isPeriodic);

parameters.name: controlPoints
parameters.description: The output array of control point that define the path of the spline.

parameters.name: degree
parameters.description: The output degree of curvature of the spline.

parameters.name: knots
parameters.description: The output array of numbers that define the knot vector of the spline.

parameters.name: isRational
parameters.description: The output value indicating if the spline is rational. A rational spline will have a weight value for each control point.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: The output array of numbers that define the weights for the spline.

parameters.name: isPeriodic
parameters.description: The output value indicating if the spline is Periodic. A periodic curve has a start point and end point that meet (with curvature continuity) forming a closed loop.

merge.title: NurbsCurve3D.merge Method
merge.description: Define a new NURBS curve that is the result of combining this NURBS curve with another NURBS curve.

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var.merge(nurbsCurve)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var->merge(nurbsCurve);

parameters.name: nurbsCurve
parameters.description: The NURBS curve to combine with.

reverse.title: NurbsCurve3D.reverse Method
reverse.description: Reverses the orientation of the curve so the start and end points are swapped. The shape of the curve remains unchanged. This is especially useful to prepare the curves to use with the merge method.

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var.reverse()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var->reverse();

set.title: NurbsCurve3D.set Method
set.description: Sets the data that defines a transient 3D NURBS rational b-spline object.

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var.set(controlPoints, degree, knots, isRational, weights, isPeriodic)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var->set(controlPoints, degree, knots, isRational, weights, isPeriodic);

parameters.name: controlPoints
parameters.description: The array of control point that define the path of the spline.

parameters.name: degree
parameters.description: The degree of curvature of the spline.

parameters.name: knots
parameters.description: An array of numbers that define the knot vector of the spline.

parameters.name: isRational
parameters.description: A bool value indicating if the spline is rational. A rational spline must have a weight value for each control point.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: An array of numbers that define the weights for the spline.

parameters.name: isPeriodic
parameters.description: A bool indicating if the spline is Periodic. A periodic curve has a start point and end point that meet (with curvature continuity) forming a closed loop.

transformBy.title: NurbsCurve3D.transformBy Method

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var.transformBy(matrix)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsCurve3D_var->transformBy(matrix);

transformBy.return_description: Return true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the curve.
